commands.review_summary:
The PR threads Discord `botUserId` into message context building so bot-authored reply targets keep reply metadata while omitting quote body/media from the agent prompt, and adds a regression test.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +58. Total +61 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, though I did not execute a live Discord run: current main maps any visible Discord reply quote body into `ReplyToBody` and fetches referenced media without checking whether the referenced author is the bot. The PR's regression test matches that source path.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair lane is appropriate because the patch is focused and the remaining blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof plus normal maintainer review.

target.title: fix(discord): suppress self-reply prompt echoes
target.body:
## Summary
- suppress Discord reply-target body/media prompt context when the referenced message was authored by the current bot user
- preserve the reply target id/sender so routing metadata still knows which message was replied to
- add a regression test for users replying to a bot's previous message without feeding that previous body back into the model

## Verification
- `/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts --testNamePattern "does not inject the bot's previous message body"`
- `/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts --testNamePattern "processDiscordMessage"`
- `/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin/node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`

Note: the first commit attempt hook hit pnpm dependency-layout enforcement in this fresh worktree because `node_modules` was a symlink to the hydrated checkout, so the final commit used `--no-verify` after the commands above passed directly.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
